title: The Briefing
tagline: AI intelligence for Gauntlet AI — April 15, 2026

items.headline: Anthropic hits $30B ARR as Claude Managed Agents launches
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Managed Agents — a platform with pre-built agent harness, sandboxed execution, multi-hour cloud runtime, and monitoring dashboards. Notion demoed it running client onboarding workflows. The launch coincides with Anthropic hitting $30 billion in annualized recurring revenue, triple its December figure, as it races OpenAI toward potential IPOs.

items.headline: Nvidia's Ising models bring AI to quantum computing
items.summary: Nvidia released Ising, a family of open AI models targeting two quantum computing bottlenecks: processor calibration and error correction. The calibration model cuts calibration time from days to hours; decoding models run 2.5x faster and 3x more accurately than pyMatching. Partners include Fermilab, Harvard, IQM Quantum Computers, and the UK's National Physical Laboratory.

items.headline: Meta's Muse Spark: a closed small model for AI glasses and social apps
items.summary: Meta announced Muse Spark, a small LLM powering an upgraded Meta AI assistant with two modes (quick answers + deep reasoning) and parallel subagent launches. It features strong multimodal perception built for Meta's AI glasses. Launching on AI glasses in the US immediately, expanding to Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, and WhatsApp in coming weeks.

items.headline: OpenAI's GPT-5.4-Cyber: a security model too hot for public release
items.summary: OpenAI expanded its Trusted Access for Cyber (TAC) program to thousands of vetted security professionals. The expansion includes GPT-5.4-Cyber — fine-tuned for defensive cybersecurity with lowered refusals and binary reverse engineering capabilities. Codex Security has already fixed 3,000+ critical and high-severity vulnerabilities across the ecosystem.

items.headline: GitHub Copilot's Rubber Duck: GPT-5.4 reviews Claude's coding decisions
items.summary: GitHub introduced Rubber Duck, an experimental Copilot CLI feature that uses a cross-family model to review coding agent decisions before execution. When Claude is primary, GPT-5.4 independently flags architectural flaws, edge cases, and missed assumptions. Testing on SWE-Bench Pro: Claude Sonnet + Rubber Duck closed 74.7% of the performance gap to Claude Opus alone on 70+ step problems.

items.headline: DeepLearning.AI: Spec-Driven Development with JetBrains — free new course
items.summary: DeepLearning.AI launched a free course on Spec-Driven Development with Coding Agents, built with JetBrains and taught by Paul Everitt. You'll learn to write project constitutions and feature specs that preserve context across agent sessions, apply a plan–implement–verify workflow, and package reusable agent skills. A 7-day Tamagotchi-style build challenge is open through April 22.

items.headline: Andrew Ng: software engineering jobs are rising, not falling — because of AI
items.summary: In his latest Batch letter, Andrew Ng cited a Citadel Research report showing software engineering job postings rising rapidly despite (or because of) coding agents. Ng argues this is encouraging: if software engineering is a harbinger of AI's impact on other professions, expansion here is a positive signal for labor markets overall.

items.headline: OpenAI attacker charged — could face domestic terrorism prosecution
items.summary: A 20-year-old Texas man, Daniel Moreno-Gama, was arrested after throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's San Francisco home and attempting to set fire to OpenAI's headquarters. He carried a self-authored anti-AI manifesto. U.S. Attorney Craig Missakian said domestic terrorism charges may apply; the explosives count alone carries 5–20 years mandatory minimum.

items.headline: AI Dev 26 × San Francisco — April 28–29 at Pier 48
items.summary: Andrew Ng and DeepLearning.AI are hosting AI Dev 26 in San Francisco, a two-day developer conference April 28–29. Speakers and engineers from Google, AMD, Oracle, Neo4j, Snowflake, and more. 3,000+ engineers, researchers, and builders. Technical talks, hands-on workshops, and live demos. Special discount tickets available.

items.headline: Claude's Mythos Preview model limited due to security concerns
items.summary: Claude's advanced Mythos Preview model raised security concerns, leading Anthropic to restrict it to limited-release only. The Batch covered this alongside Google's AlphaGenome unveiling 'dark DNA' that regulates genetic expression, and a new dynamic fluids model that solved transformers' pixellation problem.
